name: PR-C 141 South Cervera Circuit
description: Hike the 5.3-mile (8.6 km) moderate circular trail around Cervera, Spain, with 581 feet (177 metres) of elevation gain, taking about 2 hours
region_intro: Hiking around Cervera offers diverse and rugged natural landscapes, characterized by an irregular terrain that provides a serene environment for nature enthusiasts. The region features a mix of natural and cultivated areas, showcasing ancient olive trees, holm oaks, and carob trees. Hikers can explore a varied natural heritage, including native species and clear springs. Best hiking trails around Cervera The most popular hiking route is Ciudad de Cervera — circular, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete, offering views of the town and surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is University of Cervera loop from Cervera, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.1 km) path, passing historical landmarks and cultivated areas. Local hikers also love the Ermita de Sant Ramon Nonat – Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat loop from Sant Ramon, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through historical and religious sites, often completed in about 2 hours 0 minutes. Hiking around Cervera is defined by diverse natural and cultivated landscapes, ancient olive trees, and well-signposted trails. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Cervera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cervera?
A: Cervera offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 90 trails available on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Cervera?
A: Yes, Cervera has 20 easy routes perfect for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17548008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Ramon Nonat – Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat loop from Sant Ramon&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.4 km long and takes about 2 hours to complete. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31818674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jaume de la Manresana – Ermita de Santa Madrona loop from Sant Ramon&lt;/a&gt;, covering 5 km.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Cervera?
A: Cervera boasts diverse and rugged natural landscapes, featuring an irregular terrain with a mix of natural and cultivated areas. You'll encounter ancient olive trees, holm oaks, carob trees, and a rich natural heritage with native species and clear springs. The region's trails offer changing vistas and peaceful surroundings.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cervera?
A: Yes, many routes in Cervera are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1134035821/ciudad-de-cervera-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad de Cervera — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.6 km trail that offers views of the town and surrounding countryside. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;University of Cervera loop from Cervera&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1 km path passing historical landmarks.

Q: What are some notable natural features or attractions to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can discover places of incomparable beauty, including natural landscapes with native species, crystal-clear fountains and springs, and monumental trees like ancient olive trees and unique holm oaks. The region also features emblematic dry stone constructions such as huts and terraces, showcasing ancient agricultural practices.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest accessible via hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. Cervera is rich in history. You can explore the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera&lt;/a&gt; itself, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Sant Ramon Nonat&lt;/a&gt;. Several castles are also nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3066003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de l'Aranyó&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Montcortés&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Cervera?
A: The hiking trails around Cervera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-signposted trails, the diverse scenery, and the serene environment that allows for a peaceful connection with nature.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Cervera well-marked?
A: Yes, the hiking trails in Cervera are exceptionally well-signposted, making them accessible and easy to navigate for visitors. This ensures a smooth and enjoyable hiking experience.

Q: What kind of wildlife and flora can I expect to see?
A: Cervera is an ideal spot for nature lovers. You can enjoy the aromatic scents of rosemary and thyme, and observe local fauna such as birds, rabbits, foxes, reptiles, and wild boar. The region's natural heritage includes native species and monumental trees.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Cervera offers 5 difficult routes. These trails typically feature more significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, providing a rewarding experience for experienced hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cervera?
A: The diverse landscapes of Cervera can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with vibrant flora in spring and beautiful colors in autumn. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic views on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, several trails offer excellent viewpoints. For instance, the El Colomer trail crosses Mount Colomer and provides panoramic views of the municipality from its scenic viewpoint. You can also find &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5072094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Albió&lt;/a&gt;, which is a highlight in the area.
tips: The city is characterized by the richness of its cultural and monumental heritage. A total of eleven monuments in the municipality are listed as Cultural Assets of National Interest (BCIN) by the Generalitat of Catalonia: the castle (11th century), the church of San Pedro el Grande (11th century), the walled enclosure (16th century) , the parish of Santa María (14th-15th centuries), the Paeria building (17th-18th centuries), the University (18th century), the historic center, the Syndicate (20th century) and the castles of the Castellnou added nuclei of Olujas, la Preñanosa and Malgrat.

The Tossal de les Forques is a mountain of 562 meters and is located at the eastern end of Cervera. There are some rocks at the base of the tossal where there are carved sculptures and inscriptions with religious motifs, which are believed to have been carved by seminarians.
At this point on the GR 171 you have a beautiful panoramic view of the city of Cervera dominating the horizon among infinite cereal fields.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and length of the Ciudad de Cervera — circular trail?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as moderate, covering a distance of approximately 8.6 kilometers. It typically takes around 2 hours and 25 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: Where does the Ciudad de Cervera — circular trail start?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route around the town of Cervera. While a specific starting point isn't detailed, you can generally begin your hike from any accessible point within or on the outskirts of Cervera itself, as it loops back to its origin.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Ciudad de Cervera — circular route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll experience the charm of Cervera, a historical site. You'll also pass by Tossal de les Forques, a summit offering excellent Views of Cervera and the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: Is the Ciudad de Cervera — circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails around towns like Cervera are dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through urban areas, agricultural land, or near livestock. Ensure you carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route largely depends on personal preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon start is recommended. Winter hikes are possible, but check local conditions for any potential cold snaps or precipitation.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ciudad de Cervera — circular trail. It's a publicly accessible route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ciudad de Cervera — circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain for this moderate hike includes varied paths typical of a route around a town and its immediate surroundings. Expect a mix of tracks and trails, with some elevation changes as you ascend to viewpoints like Tossal de les Forques.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the 'Cervera Sud' (51%) and 'Via cicloturística InterCatalunya - Etapa 02' (47%). It also crosses parts of 'Del Santuari de Pinós als Ports' (44%) and the 'Camí de Sant Jaume (l'Hospitalet - Tàrrega)' (34%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or pubs near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the route circles Cervera, you'll find various amenities within the town itself, including cafes, restaurants, and pubs, perfect for a refreshment before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and 8.6 km length, this route might be suitable for active families with older children who are accustomed to hiking. For younger children, consider shorter sections or alternative, easier trails in the area.

faqs.question: What is the elevation gain and loss on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features an elevation gain of approximately 177 meters and a similar elevation loss of about 178 meters, contributing to its moderate difficulty rating.
